---
title: "ProSIFT Module 03: Normalization"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: default
---

**Last Updated:** 2026-03-27
**Module Status:** Stub -- not yet designed
**Pipeline Position:** [Module 02: Pre-Normalization QC/EDA] --> **Module 03: Normalization** --> [Module 04: Imputation]

---

# 1. Purpose and Scope

This module normalizes the log-transformed abundance data to correct for systematic technical biases between samples (differences in sample loading, instrument drift, etc.). Normalization runs after pre-normalization QC (Module 02) so that the raw distributional properties are already documented.

Post-normalization diagnostic plots are produced here so that normalization effectiveness can be evaluated before imputation begins.

**[FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]** Full design of this module is pending. Normalization method selection, log transformation logic, post-normalization diagnostic output spec, and process specifications need to be defined.

---

# 2. Inputs and Outputs

## 2.1 Inputs

- Filtered abundance matrix (Parquet, from Module 01 Process 4.4)
- Validated metadata (Parquet, from Module 01)
- params.yml

## 2.2 Outputs

- Normalized abundance matrix (Parquet)
- Post-normalization diagnostic plots (for QC Report Assembly, Module 04b)

---

# 3. Sub-Workflow Diagram

*[To be designed.]*

---

# 4. Process Specifications

*[To be designed. Will cover:]*

- Log2 transformation (conditional on `input.abundance_type`)
- Normalization method application (median centering, quantile, VSN)
- Post-normalization distribution and sample-correlation diagnostics

---

# 5. Design Decisions

*[No module-specific decisions recorded yet.]*

---

# 6. Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `normalization.method` | string | "median" | median, quantile, vsn, or none |
| `normalization.log_transform` | bool | true | Apply log2 if `input.abundance_type` is "raw" |

---

# 7. Known Limitations and Edge Cases

- Normalization assumes that most proteins are not differentially abundant (the majority rule). When a large fraction of proteins are truly changed between conditions, standard normalization methods can introduce bias.
- VSN is a variance-stabilizing transformation that normalizes and log-transforms simultaneously; it should not be combined with a separate log2 step.

---

# 8. Testing Notes

*[To be populated during implementation.]*

---

# 9. Change History

### 2026-03-27 -- Split from 03_normalization_imputation.Rmd

**Change:** Module file created by splitting the combined normalization/imputation stub into separate module files (03_normalization.Rmd and 04_imputation.Rmd) to match the revised pipeline architecture.
**Reason:** Normalization and imputation are sequential but distinct steps with different diagnostic outputs and design considerations. Separating them allows each to have its own Nextflow process, parameter namespace, and output spec.
**Affected processes:** N/A (stub only).
**Downstream effects:** Module numbers 04-08 renumbered to 06-10.
